name: Boron Nitride Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Boron Nitride. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for ceramic cleaning

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for effective cleaning resolution

repetitionRate.description: Pulse frequency for controlled material removal

energyDensity.description: Energy per unit area for surface cleaning

pulseWidth.description: Pulse duration for minimal thermal penetration

scanSpeed.description: Beam travel speed for uniform coverage

passCount.description: Number of cleaning cycles for complete removal

overlapRatio.description: Beam overlap for consistent surface treatment

power.description: Typical range for pulsed Nd:YAG lasers in ceramic cleaning; starting at 50 W to avoid thermal damage to BN's high melting point substrate (~3000°C). Max safe below damage threshold. Source: IPG Photonics specs for cleaning systems; conservative for BN due to limited specific studies.

scan_speed.description: Effective range for contamination removal; recommended 1000 mm/s for optimal balance of cleaning efficiency and heat control on BN. Light contamination: 1500-3000 mm/s; heavy: 100-500 mm/s. Source: Industry standards from Laserax and Adapt Laser manuals.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for industrial cleaning to ablate contaminants with minimal HAZ; 20 ns recommended for BN to leverage its thermal conductivity and reduce heat accumulation. Picosecond (ps) or femtosecond for ultra-precision but less common industrially. Source: Laser physics reviews in Optics & Lasers in Engineering (e.g., studies on ceramic ablation).

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed cleaning; 30 kHz recommended for BN to balance speed and prevent heat buildup, as higher rates increase average power and risk substrate damage. Trade-off: High freq accelerates cleaning but accumulates heat in low-absorptivity materials like BN at 1064 nm. Source: Academic literature in Journal of Applied Physics on pulse train effects.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant ablation; damage threshold for BN estimated ~2-5 J/cm² based on ceramic studies, so 0.5 J/cm² recommended to stay below while effective for surface prep. Conservative due to BN's reflectivity at 1064 nm. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Applied Surface Science (e.g., on nitrides and ceramics).

spot_size.description: Typical beam diameter for cleaning; 200 μm recommended for BN to provide fine control on detailed surfaces without excessive energy concentration. Fine detail: 100-200 μm; large area: 300-500 μm. Source: Equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems for industrial cleaning.

pass_count.description: Typical for complete removal in multi-pass to ensure uniform cleaning without single-pass overload; 2 passes recommended for BN contamination to minimize risk to substrate integrity. Single pass for light layers. Source: Industry case studies in laser cleaning of ceramics (e.g., SPIE proceedings).

overlap_ratio.description: Standard for uniform coverage; 50% recommended for BN to achieve even cleaning while avoiding overexposure and heat zones. Range adjusts for contamination density. Source: Process optimization in Journal of Laser Applications and manufacturer guidelines (e.g., CleanLaser).
